channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Several serum proteins may provide prognostic value in metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease
item.description: 1. Distinct circulating and hepatic molecular signatures appear to track metabolic dysfunction-associated steatotic liver disease progression. 2. Seru...

item.title: Meghan Trainor on Building Confidence and Healthy Habits
item.description: Meghan shares how she’s tuning out criticism, prioritizing her health, and building habits that help her feel stronger than ever.

item.title: What Happens to Your Body When You Drink Enough Water
8 Ways to Get the Protein You Need
Study finds children in the US die at higher rates than peers in other high-income nations
item.description: At every age, children in the United States die at higher rates than peers in other high-income nations, according to a new study from Children's Hosp...

item.title: Sweet discovery rewrites understanding of how our bodies store sugar
item.description: WEHI researchers have discovered a never-before-seen mechanism our bodies use to regulate sugar, in findings that rewrite the fundamental rules of bio...

item.title: HBP May Pose Major Risks for Young Veterans Post-9/11
item.description: A new trend emerging in post-9/11 veterans shows that roughly half of this young adult group has hypertension, and more than half of those cases go un...

item.title: New NHS Standards Target Leading Causes of Maternal Death
item.description: New clinical standards from NHS England targeting blood clots, mental health, haemorrhage, and epilepsy aim to cut maternal deaths by March 2027. Meds...

item.title: ICIs Show High Responses in Mediastinal B-Cell Lymphoma
item.description: Pembrolizumab and nivolumab show favorable response rates with or without consolidation therapy. Medscape Medical News

item.title: Improving cardiovascular risk prediction in Latin America and the Caribbean: SCORE2-LAC
item.description: A preliminary model to improve the prediction of cardiovascular risk in Latin America and the Caribbean was presented at ESC Preventive Cardiology 202...

item.title: EMA Recommends Generic in Advanced/Metastatic Breast Cancer
item.description: Palbociclib slows cancer cell proliferation in patients with HR+/HER2- locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer. Medscape News Europe

item.title: EMA Greenlights Drugs for MS and Spinal Muscular Atrophy
item.description: Cenrifki prevents MS disease progression and Itvisma delivers a healthy gene-to-cell nuclei, addressing the root cause of SMA. Medscape News Europe

item.title: Alcohol causes more cancers in Australia than previously thought
item.description: Alcohol consumption is likely to cause more cancer incidence in Australia than previously thought, according to new research from the University of Sy...

item.title: Mechanical forces from the beating heart may help prevent cancer cell growth
item.description: Scientists may have discovered another way the human body tries to protect itself from cancer. New research on mice suggests that the heart's constant...

item.title: TikTok “Fibermaxxing” trend targets gut microbiome health
item.description: 1. The viral “Fibermaxxing” trend prioritizes high-volume whole-food fiber intake to optimize gut microbiome diversity and improve metabolic satiety. ...

item.title: Small-bites fascial closure reduces long-term risk of incisional hernia after midline laparotomy
item.description: 1. The small-bites fascial closure technique reduced the long-term incidence of incisional hernias compared to the traditional large-bites technique a...

item.title: Increased childhood intake of ultra-processed foods is associated with a higher body mass index in young adulthood
item.description: 1. Greater childhood intake of ultra-processed foods was associated with higher body mass index in young adulthood. 2. The association was most eviden...

item.title: How to treat chronic pain and depression together

item.title: How research laboratory culture shapes mentorship in academic life
item.description: Every research mentor eventually faces a difficult question: What, and who, will continue when the work is no longer theirs to carry forward? We often...

item.title: Transforming sepsis care with rapid host response diagnostics
item.description: In busy emergency departments, sepsis is often discussed as something we “cannot miss” rather than a diagnosis we can confidently make. Patients may a...

item.title: Why early detection technology and precision medicine are failing patients
item.description: The tools to detect serious disease years before symptoms appear already exist. What we are waiting on is a health care system structured to use them....

item.title: The Scan by 2 Minute Medicine®: TikTok “Fibermaxxing” trend targets gut microbiome diversity, Jennifer Aniston’s “Strength for 50+” ritual spotlights photobiomodulation, American Lung Association issues respiratory warning over viral “mouth taping” fad, and Psychiatric Times highlights initiative to close the schizophrenia mortality gap
